note: Per-DIRECTION ratchet floors for PRODUCTION (non-test/fixture) sharded≡exact RESOLVED-edge divergences on the unified resolution model (ADR-0033, supersedes ADR-0032). ALL FLOORS ARE 0: after convergence the invariant is ZERO production divergence — any phantom (sharded-only), decline (exact-only), or conflict (both-resolve-differently) edge fails graph-equivalence-check, as does any functionsOnly{Exact,Sharded} or SCC divergence. Both engines run ONE model: the single-program (exact) build recovers cross-package edges via the SAME boundary linker (resolveCrossBoundaryCalls) the sharded build runs post-merge (exact = the 1-shard case). DIVERGENCE JOURNEY: 204 (pre-plan) -> 12 (Phase 3 convergence) -> 1 (chained-call position fix: calleeAnchorNode anchors edges at the callee token, de-colliding recv().method()) -> 0 (intra-package .d.ts->source method pin: pinByDtsDeclSource in resolve-decl.ts maps a checker-attested dist/*.d.ts method decl to its SOURCE occurrence and pins by file+name, RESTRICTED to intra-package targets so both engines resolve it in-shard — fixes exact under-resolving a method whose receiver type flows through the owner package's own published .d.ts, e.g. dashboard-data.ts:44 scope.graph?.rules.getAll(); the unsound shard-scoped unique-name catalog fallback no longer drives the result). CROSS-PACKAGE METHOD CALLS are now resolved through the boundary linker in BOTH engines (type-attested method boundary calls: the checker maps recv.m()'s decl in a workspace dist/*.d.ts to its source, carried as CrossBoundaryCall.targetFile, pinned post-merge by (targetFile + name) unique-or-decline — methodTargetFile + the resolveOne method-target branch). ~185 such edges resolve on this repo (symmetric, divergence stays 0); ~208 decline (unique-or-decline: ambiguous name-in-file or a dist→src mapping with no occurrence) and stay both-declined. KNOWN OUT-OF-SCOPE (both engines decline → invisible to this differential gate, guarded by the pinned-corpus resolution-completeness floor in resolution-completeness-floor.test.ts): arrow-property method calls like logger.info() where findCatalogEntry cannot reproduce the arrow occurrence's hash (the ones that do resolve via method-target now do; the rest decline). DEFERRED (explicit): clean-checkout parity for WORKSPACE imports — exact still resolves @scope/pkg through the dep's built dist/*.d.ts to reach the boundary linker; relative imports are clean-safe (file+name pin). Source-as-surface deferred; Phase 6's clean-checkout assertion is scoped to the relative class.
